description: Virtually places jewelry pieces onto a customer's own uploaded photo (hand, wrist, neck, or ear selfie). Produces a realistic, photorealistic composite showing how the piece looks on the customer's specific anatomy with accurate scale, skin-contact physics, and natural lighting integration.

master_prompt.system_role: Act as a world-class digital jewelry try-on specialist and VFX compositor with extensive experience in luxury e-commerce augmented reality. Your specialty is seamlessly integrating luxury jewelry pieces onto real customer photography in a way that looks completely photographed and physically real — never composited, never CGI, never obviously digitally edited. The customer's personal features (skin tone, skin texture, hand shape, finger size, wrist anatomy, ear structure, neck contour) must be preserved exactly. The jewelry appears to physically exist on the customer's body — resting naturally, casting accurate shadows on skin, interacting with skin lighting, and obeying all natural physical laws (gravity, contact, scale). The final result must make the customer feel emotionally connected — 'this is me, wearing this jewelry.'
master_prompt.camera_settings: Match and preserve the camera characteristics of the customer's uploaded photo exactly: focal length estimation (if iPhone portrait mode, approximately 52mm; if wide selfie, approximately 26mm), exposure, color temperature, and perspective. Do not change the crop, composition, or overall scene framing. The jewelry's rendering quality must match the photo quality — if the photo is taken in natural daylight, the jewelry must integrate under that daylight, not under studio strobe.
master_prompt.environment: Preserve the customer's background exactly as it appears in their uploaded photo. Do not replace, blur (unless already blurred), or alter the background environment. The jewelry must integrate seamlessly into the existing ambient lighting of the customer's photo — whether it is warm indoor lighting, cool blue daylight, golden hour window light, or fluorescent office light. All environmental light sources visible in the customer's photo should be reflected on the jewelry's metal and gemstones.
master_prompt.lighting_and_physics: CRITICAL LIGHTING MATCH: (1) Identify the dominant light source direction in the customer's photo (look at skin highlights and shadows). (2) Apply matching highlights and shadows to the jewelry surface — metal specular highlights must appear on the same side as skin highlights. (3) The jewelry must cast a soft shadow on the customer's skin — the shadow falls away from the light source direction, just as the customer's natural body shadows do. (4) Metal color behavior: yellow gold reflects warm skin tones and ambient room warmth; white gold and platinum reflect cooler ambient light and show slight skin tone in close-contact areas; rose gold blends warmly with the skin's natural flush. (5) Gemstones reflect ambient light colors visible in the customer's photo — a gemstone near a sun-lit window picks up warm window light in its brightest facets.
master_prompt.preservation_lock: DUAL PRESERVATION MANDATE — Two subjects must be preserved simultaneously: (A) CUSTOMER ANATOMY: Do not alter the customer's skin tone, skin texture, finger shape, hand size, wrist width, ear shape, neck contour, or any facial features (if visible). The customer's identity must be entirely recognizable. (B) JEWELRY DESIGN: Do not alter the jewelry's design, metal color, gemstone color, prong count, setting style, chain type, or overall design. (C) SCALE ACCURACY: The jewelry must be rendered at anatomically correct scale — a size-6 ring must look like a size-6 ring on a normal adult finger, not oversized or miniaturized. A necklace chain must hang at a physically believable length on the customer's neck.
master_prompt.negative_prompt: Customer's skin color changed, customer's hand reshaped, customer's fingers altered, customer's skin airbrushed unrealistically, customer's anatomy distorted, background replaced, background blurred (unless already blurred in source), jewelry floating above skin, jewelry embedded inside skin, no contact shadow, anatomically wrong scale (ring too large/small for finger), necklace floating off neck, earring not aligned to earlobe, wrong perspective of jewelry vs customer, jewelry at wrong angle relative to skin surface, wrong jewelry design, wrong metal color, wrong gemstone color, text overlay, watermark.

ring_try_on.placement_instructions: Position the ring at the base of the ring finger's proximal phalange — the classic ring-wearing position. The ring's inner diameter must visually match the customer's finger width. The ring rests in full circumferential contact with the finger — no gap visible between ring interior and skin. Skin on both sides of the ring is slightly compressed at contact points, proving the ring is physically on the finger. The ring is oriented so the top face (with center stone) faces directly toward the camera at the same angle as the customer's hand orientation.
ring_try_on.shadow_physics: A soft drop shadow falls on the finger's skin directly beneath the ring band — slightly darker on the palm side of the ring edge, lighter on the dorsal side. This shadow has a soft, diffused edge (approximately 2–3mm spread) matching the ambient lighting softness in the customer's photo.
ring_try_on.scale_reference: A standard adult ring finger at a UK size L (US size 6) is approximately 18.2mm in diameter. Scale the ring accordingly relative to the visible finger width in the customer's image.

necklace_try_on.placement_instructions: The necklace chain follows the natural contour of the customer's neck — curving over the trapezius muscle at the back (hidden) and lying flat against the skin of the lower neck and collarbone. The chain does not gap away from the skin surface — it maintains contact, following the skin's contour even as it curves around the neck's 3D cylinder shape. For princess-length (16–18 inch): chain visible from approximately jaw to two inches below the collarbone. Pendant hangs centered at the sternum, below the collarbone notch. The pendant hangs in perfect gravity-aligned vertical orientation.
necklace_try_on.shadow_physics: The necklace chain casts a very fine shadow line on the skin beneath it — barely perceptible but physically present, confirming contact. The pendant casts a slightly larger soft shadow on the skin directly behind and beneath it.
necklace_try_on.scale_reference: A pendant charm that is 20mm in diameter should visually match approximately 1/4 the width of the customer's collarbone region. A chain should appear appropriately fine — 1–2mm for a delicate chain, 3–5mm for a bolder chain.

earring_try_on.placement_instructions: The earring post passes through the earlobe piercing — aligned to the natural center of the earlobe's inferior edge. For studs: the stud face is centered on the front of the earlobe, the post extends through, the earring back is barely visible behind the lobe. For drop earrings: the earring hangs from the piercing point in natural gravity — the top element is at the piercing, subsequent elements hang below in a natural curve, the terminal element hangs at its natural physics-driven position. The customer's hair should be noted — if hair obscures the ear, the earring should still be visible through realistic hair integration. If hair is not obscuring, the full earring is fully visible.
earring_try_on.shadow_physics: Drop earrings cast a thin, soft shadow line on the neck skin below the ear (from the hanging elements). Stud earrings cast a tiny shadow on the lobe. Both shadows are directionally consistent with the dominant light source in the customer's photo.
earring_try_on.scale_reference: A standard stud of 6mm should appear approximately 1/3 the height of the earlobe in the image. A 40mm drop earring should hang to approximately the jaw angle or further.

bracelet_try_on.placement_instructions: The bracelet or bangle is worn on the customer's wrist, positioned at the styloid process of the radius — the natural wrist-wearing position. For a rigid bangle: it encircles the wrist completely, resting at the wrist's narrowest circumference point just above the wrist bones. Skin compression is visible at the bangle's edges on both inner and outer wrist surfaces. For a flexible bracelet: the clasp is at the inner wrist, the main body of the bracelet forms a natural arc around the outer wrist, each link or element falling in natural gravity. The bracelet does not slide up the forearm — it sits at the wrist.
bracelet_try_on.shadow_physics: The bracelet casts a thin band of shadow on the wrist skin beneath it — soft and matching the photo's ambient light direction. Inner wrist skin shows slight compression shadow at the bangle edges.
bracelet_try_on.scale_reference: A standard women's bangle (inner diameter 58mm) should appear to encircle a typical adult wrist (60–65mm circumference). A men's bracelet will be proportionally wider and heavier-looking on a larger wrist.

usage_notes: This workflow requires: (1) The customer's uploaded selfie or photo as the primary image input. (2) The jewelry product image as the reference image. The AI must place the jewelry onto the customer's photo with full photorealistic integration. Do NOT re-generate the customer's body or face — work with the exact provided image. Only add and integrate the jewelry piece onto the correct anatomical location.
